description_long: This radiator features a tall, rectangular structure with a pitched roof-like top, giving it a distinctive architectural appearance. The surface is covered in a weathered, rusted metal texture, suggesting prolonged outdoor exposure. The upper section has a large rectangular opening, possibly for ventilation or access, while the lower section is composed of evenly spaced horizontal slats that form a grille. The back and sides are solid, with the grille pattern only present on the front and rear faces. The overall design is utilitarian, with no visible decorative elements, and the construction appears robust and functional for urban environments. The object stands on four narrow legs, elevating it slightly above the ground.
description: A tall, rusted metal radiator with a pitched roof top, large upper opening, and horizontal slatted grille on the lower section. The design is robust and utilitarian, suitable for outdoor urban use.
description_view_0: Front view showing the pitched roof, large upper opening, and horizontal slatted grille below.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the solid side panels and the depth of the upper opening.
description_view_2: Rear view mirroring the front, with the same pitched roof and grille pattern visible.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, again showing the solid side and the structure of the slatted grille.
